subject: English Grammar
source: Liberia Ministry of Education - National Curriculum for Senior Secondary Schools

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to use correct nouns and pronouns in writing different types of compositions.
objectives: Construct sentences using nouns and pronouns correctly
Distinguish nouns from pronouns in different types of compositions
Use nouns and pronouns effectively in oral and written English
Define nouns and pronouns
Identify and use the kinds of nouns and pronouns correctly in spoken and written English
content: Nouns: Common and Proper, Concrete and Abstract, Singular and Plural, Compound and Collective, Countable and Uncountable
Pronouns (Antecedents of Pronouns): Personal Pronouns, Possessive Pronouns, Indefinite Pronouns, Demonstrative Pronouns, Interrogative Pronouns
activities: Assign learners to point out that words that name persons, places, things, or ideas are nouns; ask learners to list the different kinds of nouns in a given piece of work
Group learners and give at least ten words every week; assign learners into groups to emphasize that pronouns replace nouns and ask them to give examples
Discuss pronouns and their antecedents; help learners understand the more difficult concepts of pronouns standing for entire groups of words and antecedents that follow pronouns
Vocabulary Development: Learners to learn spellings and meanings and be able to use them in sentences
Review study skills and note taking
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior High Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 1 (Longman & Sedco, 2008)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools (Longman, 2008) W. Stannard Allen
Writing and Grammar — Communication in Action, Prentice Hall
Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English (5th Edition)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to effectively construct composition using different tenses of verbs; learners will use verbs correctly when speaking.
objectives: Write sentences using verbs correctly
Distinguish between visible and mental action verbs
Distinguish between transitive and intransitive verbs
Identify helping verbs and how they are used
Use the different tenses of verbs correctly
content: Verbs Part 1: Action Verbs — Transitive and Intransitive
Tenses of Verbs (present, past and future)
activities: Assign learners to construct sentences with appropriate action verbs and point out the action words, whether visible or mental
Divide learners into small groups to identify transitive verbs in sentences and the objects that receive the action of the verb; emphasize that intransitive verbs do not
Assign learners to distinguish between the main and helping verbs in passages
Assign learners to construct sentences using the three verb tenses
Help learners go through the writing process and develop given topics into descriptive essays
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior High Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 1 (Longman & Sedco, 2008)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools (Longman, 2008) W. Stannard Allen
Writing and Grammar — Communication in Action, Prentice Hall
Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English (5th Edition)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to construct different types of essays using the three verb tenses, regular and irregular verbs correctly.
objectives: Utilize the perfect tenses of verbs correctly
Distinguish between regular and irregular verbs and how they form their past tenses
Write persuasive essays using the relevant mechanics
content: Verbs Part 2: Tenses (present perfect, past perfect and future perfect tenses)
activities: Assign learners to explain and demonstrate the perfect tenses of verbs
Group learners to construct sentences using the perfect tenses
Assign learners to list regular and irregular verbs discussing how their past tense is formed
Group learners to construct sentences using regular and irregular verbs in their present and past tenses
Group learners to do persuasive writing and discuss the issues involved for presentation
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior High Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 1 (Longman & Sedco, 2008)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools (Longman, 2008) W. Stannard Allen
Writing and Grammar — Communication in Action, Prentice Hall
Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English (5th Edition)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to demonstrate proper use of Adjectives and Adverbs in compositions.
objectives: Distinguish adjectives from other parts of speech and explain how they modify nouns and pronouns
Apply adjectives and adverbs appropriately to make writing vivid or precise
Recognize adverbs and how they modify verbs, adjectives, and adverbs
Debate the four questions adverbs answer (Where? When? In what way? To what extent?)
Discuss adjectives and ask questions such as what kind, which one, how much, or how many
activities: Learners identify adjectives and the nouns or pronouns they modify
Learners write adjectives and adverbs in speech and writings
Learners classify adjectives and adverbs
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior High Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 1 (Longman & Sedco, 2008)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools (Longman, 2008) W. Stannard Allen
Writing and Grammar — Communication in Action, Prentice Hall
Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English (5th Edition)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to effectively demonstrate the correct use of Prepositions, Conjunctions, and Interjections in written and spoken English.
objectives: Establish that a prepositional phrase consists of a preposition and a noun or pronoun
Distinguish prepositions with their objects from adverbs
Pinpoint coordinating, subordinating, and correlative conjunctions
Properly use conjunctive adverbs
Demonstrate that interjections express feelings or emotions and function independently of a sentence
content: Prepositions: Prepositions and Prepositional phrase, Telling Prepositions and Adverbs apart
Conjunctions: Coordinating conjunctions, Correlative conjunctions, Subordinating conjunctions, Conjunctive adverbs
activities: Assign learners in groups to discuss that prepositions perform the important task of relating words in a sentence; they show relationships including location, direction, cause, and possession
Learners identify prepositions in sentences and point out the relationships shown
Teacher writes sentences on the board omitting the conjunctions and have learners supply them
Learners discuss the functions of conjunctions
Divide learners in groups to identify the different types of conjunctions and provide examples
Learners discuss what a conjunctive adverb is, list examples, and explain the correlation between two ideas (contrast, similarity, emphasis)
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior High Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 1 (Longman & Sedco, 2008)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools, W. Stannard Allen

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to demonstrate the ability to use correct English phrases in written and spoken English.
objectives: Recognize kinds of phrases in sentences
Distinguish prepositional phrases from adjective and adverb phrases in sentences
Identify appositives and appositive phrases and the words they name
Apply participle and participial phrases in sentences
Identify gerunds and gerund phrases in sentences
Construct sentences using infinitives and infinitive phrases
Compare and contrast independent and subordinate clauses
content: Verbal Phrases: Participle Phrase, Gerund Phrase, Infinitive Phrase
Phrasal Verbs: Verb followed by a preposition or adverb (e.g., ran across, broke down, get away)
Clauses: Independent Clauses, Dependent Clauses, Noun Clauses, Adjective Clauses, Adverb Clauses
activities: Learners discuss kinds of phrases and provide examples
Learners differentiate between noun phrases and verb phrases
Learners brainstorm and generate examples of various phrases
Learners explain what appositives are, and identify them in sentences; give examples of appositive phrases
Organize learners into groups to discuss gerunds, infinitives, and participles in various sentences with examples
Learners classify verbal phrases that function as noun, adjective, or adverb
Learners write comparison-and-contrast essay; learners should develop topics
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior High Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 1 (Longman & Sedco, 2008)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools (Longman, 2008) W. Stannard Allen
Writing and Grammar — Communication in Action, Prentice Hall
Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English (5th Edition)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to apply previous knowledge gained to use verbs correctly in written composition and write book reports.
objectives: Classify and use the six tenses of verb correctly, including the progressive forms
Use the five forms of verbs correctly
Write effective book reports
content: The five forms of a verb: Base form (start), s-form (starts), ing form (starting), Past tense (started), Past Participle (has/have started)
Using the six tenses of verb correctly (including the progressive forms)
Writing book reports in the context of literature
activities: Assign learners to write a cause and effect passage and state the problem
Learners discuss contemporary issues to develop topics into cause-and-effect essay
Learners read passages from any given text and identify as many verb tenses as possible; classify the identified verbs into verb tenses
Learners incorporate sentences using the six tenses of verb
Assign learners to read short stories and write book report using the standard format
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior Secondary Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 2 (Longman & Sedco, 2007)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools (Longman, 2008) W. Stannard Allen
Common Mistakes in English (Longman, 2000) T.J. Fitikides
Writing and Grammar — Communication in Action, Prentice Hall
Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English (5th Edition)

topics.topic: Grammar: Verb Usage — Active and Passive Voice
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to write effective paragraphs using passive and active voices of the verb tenses.
objectives: Classify the active and passive voices of sentences, and construct sentences effectively
Construct effective paragraphs using the elements of a paragraph
Apply proper mechanics to write sentences effectively
content: Verb usage: Proper verb usage, Active and Passive voices
Writing Paragraph: The Topic Sentence, The Body, The Conclusion, Unity, Coherence
Proofreading: Mechanics of grammar, Misspelled words, Grammatical errors (run-on sentences, sentence fragments, shift in pronouns, wrong capitalization, abbreviation)
activities: Learners review previous lesson on the verb tenses
Learners write the past tense of given irregular verbs
Learners construct verbs whose present principal part ends in 'y' by adding 'i' to form the third person singular
Learners read short paragraphs and identify the main idea; develop topic sentences and supporting details using current events topics
Compose effective paragraphs explicitly stating an opinion
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior Secondary Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Books 1, 2 & 3 (Longman & Sedco, 2007)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools (Longman, 2008) W. Stannard Allen
Common Mistakes in English (Longman, 2000) T.J. Fitikides
Writing and Grammar — Communication in Action, Prentice Hall
Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English (5th Edition)

topics.topic: Grammar: Verb Usage — Part 3 and Essay Writing
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to demonstrate more proficient use of verbs in spoken and written English by writing proper expositions.
objectives: Use verbs in the past progressive, present simple, past simple, past continuous, and future tenses
Identify the thesis statement of the essay
Use the dictionary effectively in defining words
content: Verb usage Part 3: Using past continuous, Using present simple, Past simple, Using past continuous
Writing Essays: Selecting writing topics, The thesis statement, The Organization of the Essay, Unity, Coherence, Mechanics
Dictionary skills: Words arranged in alphabetical order, Using guide words, Finding the meaning of each word, Parts of speech
activities: Assign learners in groups to discuss thesis statement and have them identify the central idea as the thesis statement of a paragraph
Learners must identify supporting ideas in each body part that relates to the thesis statement
Learners must identify the concluding paragraph
Learners give a series of topics; formulate thesis statements on various topics
Learners demonstrate usage of the dictionary
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior High Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 2 (Longman & Sedco, 2008)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools (Longman, 2008) W. Stannard Allen
Writing and Grammar — Communication in Action, Prentice Hall
Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English (5th Edition)

learning_outcomes: Learners are able to demonstrate proper use of words with similar meanings, opposite meanings, prefixes, suffixes, and root words in compositions.
objectives: Explain the differences amongst synonyms, antonyms, and homophones
Define prefix, root, and suffix to learn words
Apply spelling rules
content: Prefixes and meaning: Word Analysis, Root and Meaning, Affixes
Vocabulary and Spelling: Using Context Clues, Spelling Rules
activities: Learners discuss Synonyms, Antonyms, Homophones, and Homographs and indicate examples of each
Learners review and discuss root words and use context clues to arrive at word meanings
Learners review commonly misspelled words for proper spellings
Learners take turns constructing sentences using context clues
Learners generate a list of synonyms, homographs, antonyms and homophones
Learners write words with prefixes and suffixes
Learners explore the use of roots as a way of finding out the meanings of unfamiliar words
Learners review and discuss the difference between base words and root words
Learners analyze word parts to distinguish meaning
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior High Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 2 (Longman & Sedco, 2008)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools (Longman, 2008) W. Stannard Allen
Common Mistakes in English (Longman, 2000) T.J. Fitikides
Writing and Grammar — Communication in Action, Prentice Hall
Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English (5th Edition)

topics.topic: Grammar: Library Skills and Reading Comprehension
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to demonstrate proper use of Card Catalog to find materials in library to develop critical thinking and reading skills.
objectives: Use the card catalog or online catalog to find information in the library
Provide correct answers to comprehension and critical thinking questions from given text
Apply the various reading methods to get meaning from passages
content: Library Skills: Using Card Catalog/Online Catalog to find materials in the library, Call number, Information in the catalog, Materials in the Library
Reading Comprehension: Answering reading comprehension questions, Direct questions, Critical thinking questions
Active Reading: Activity before reading, Activity when reading, Activity after reading
Reading Methods: Scanning, Skimming, SQ4R Method (Survey, Question, Read, Record, Recite, Review)
activities: Group learners to demonstrate the use of card catalog to find materials in the library
Divide learners into groups to set up a library in the class with a librarian and prepare card catalog with poster sheet and have learners role play to find materials
Assign learners to organize a class visit to the nearest library as a follow-up
Learners read passages and answer comprehension questions
Learners discuss and explain reading methods listed in the content
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior High Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 2 (Longman & Sedco, 2008)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools (Longman, 2008) W. Stannard Allen
Common Mistakes in English (Longman, 2000) T.J. Fitikides
Writing and Grammar — Communication in Action, Prentice Hall
Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English (5th Edition)

topics.topic: Grammar: Letter Writing, Speech Writing, and Report Writing
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to use the parts of formal letter writing to appropriately write business letter, letter of application, excuse, request, and thank you.
Learners will write speeches as well as reports based on prior knowledge gained.
objectives: Distinguish the five parts of formal and informal letters
Pinpoint acceptable forms of letter writing
content: Letter Writing: Informal Letter, Formal/Business Letter, Letter of Application, Letter of Excuse/Request/Thanks
Listening to Speeches and Oral Presentations for Critical Evaluation
activities: Learners explain parts of formal and informal letters
Divide learners in groups to construct different kinds of writings: Business, Application, Excuse, Request, Speech, and Report
Learners listen to colleagues as they present for critical evaluation
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior High Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 2 (Longman & Sedco, 2008)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools (Longman, 2008) W. Stannard Allen
Writing and Grammar — Communication in Action, Prentice Hall
Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English (5th Edition)

topics.topic: Grammar: The Three Cases of Pronouns and Verb Usage
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to write incorporating the cases of pronouns and perfect tenses of verbs.
objectives: Construct speeches effectively using the perfect tenses
Differentiate the three cases of pronouns
Recognize different kinds of speeches
Prepare and present speeches
Summarize passages
content: The three Pronoun cases: Nominative, Objective and Possessive
Verb Usage: Perfect tenses, Present Perfect, Present Perfect Progressive, Past Perfect, Past Perfect Progressive, Future Perfect, Future Perfect Progressive
activities: Divide learners in groups to review and discuss the three cases: Nominative, Objective, and Possessive — provide examples
In a contrived scenario, learners are asked to use the appropriate tense
Group learners to read and summarize given texts
Assign learners to read different kinds of speeches (informative, persuasive, entertaining and extemporaneous), and distinguish identifying characteristics
Divide learners into groups to develop and present speeches regularly on: Socio-economic issues, Risky behaviors, Teenage pregnancies, Drug and Alcohol Abuse, Gender Based Violence
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior High Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 3 (Longman & Sedco, 2008)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools (Longman, 2008) W. Stannard Allen
Writing and Grammar — Communication in Action, Prentice Hall
Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English (5th Edition)

topics.topic: Grammar: Poem, Prose, Drama, Speech Writing and Review of Verb Usage
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to prepare and present original work of art such as poems, short stories, and different kinds of speeches.
objectives: Create an original work of art such as a poem, a story, or a drama
Demonstrate effective speech writing skills
Recognize different kinds of speeches
Compose and present speeches
content: Five forms/principal parts of a verb
Tenses: Present simple, Past Simple, Future simple, Present perfect, Past Perfect, Future Perfect
activities: Divide learners into groups to write poems, plays or short stories using their imaginations and creativity
Group learners to have more practice in using the tenses
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior High Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 3 (Longman & Sedco, 2008)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools (Longman, 2008) W. Stannard Allen
Writing and Grammar — Communication in Action, Prentice Hall
Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English (5th Edition)

topics.topic: Grammar: Phrasal Verbs, Common Mistakes and Past WASSCE Papers
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to use phrasal verbs and improve their spelling ability and the skills to write and deliver speeches.
objectives: Construct compositions using proper phrasal verbs
Demonstrate correct use of words to avoid common mistakes
Review past WASSCE Papers
content: Review common mistakes and troublesome words
activities: Learners demonstrate use of Phrasal Verbs
Learners discuss and review troublesome words and common mistakes
Assign learners to practice writing speeches and present in the classroom for critical evaluation
Assign learners to review past WASSCE Papers in preparation to be successful in public test taking
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior High Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 3 (Longman & Sedco, 2008)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson

topics.topic: Grammar: Essay Writing, Creative Writing, and Verb Usage Review
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to write and deliver different kinds of speeches.
objectives: Compose different kinds of speeches
Develop and deliver public speeches
content: Review Verb Usage: Five forms/principal parts of a verb
Tenses: Present Simple, Past Simple, Future Simple, Present Perfect, Past Perfect, Future Perfect
activities: Divide learners into groups of five in writing expositions: essay, narrative, descriptive, persuasive
Assign learners to be able to use the principal parts of a verb properly in given texts
Group learners to evaluate different kinds of writings in the classroom
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior High Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 3 (Longman & Sedco, 2008)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools (Longman, 2008) W. Stannard Allen
Writing and Grammar — Communication in Action, Prentice Hall

topics.topic: Grammar: Review Vocabulary, Phrases and Clauses
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to apply improved context clues, review of spelling rules, and the discussion and revision of phrases and clauses to construct stories, speeches and other documents.
objectives: Demonstrate the improved use of context clues in learning vocabulary
Improved application of spelling rules
content: Review vocabulary development and spelling rules
activities: Assign learners to review and discuss vocabulary development through rehearsal exercises
Group learners to solicit feedback and provide corrective measures
Divide learners to review and discuss context clues and spelling rules
Assign learners in groups of five to review and discuss Phrases and Clauses
topics.primary_text: Gateway to English for Senior High Schools, N. Grant & A B K Dadzie (Eds.), Students' Book 3 (Longman & Sedco, 2008)
secondary_texts: Senior Secondary Guide English - Pearson
Living English Structure for Schools (Longman, 2008) W. Stannard Allen
Writing and Grammar — Communication in Action, Prentice Hall
Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English (5th Edition)

topics.topic: Grammar: More Review with WASSCE Papers
learning_outcomes: Learners are able to respond positively to questions in past WASSCE Papers and make successful pass in future examinations.
objectives: Provide correct responses using past WASSCE papers
activities: Divide learners in groups to review and discuss past WASSCE Papers
Assign learners for continuous rehearsal of past WASSCE Papers to be successful in taking public examinations
